name: CERT: Rule Violation Remediated
description: Violations of CERT Rules (normative) are remediated, or covered by an approved deviation.
rationale: SEI CERT: Rules are normative requirements — a violation is a defect that must be corrected; an exception is permissible only via a documented, approved deviation.
source: SEI CERT Coding Standards (Carnegie Mellon Software Engineering Institute) — CERT C and CERT C++ Secure Coding Standards, with the CERT risk-assessment methodology (Severity × Likelihood × Remediation Cost → Priority → Level L1/L2/L3). https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/display/seccode
